seoTitle: How to Create a Knowledge Base in Zendesk (2026)
metaDescription: Build a self-service Zendesk knowledge base from scratch: activate Guide, organize categories and sections, write articles, and publish them for customers.
intro: Answering the same support questions over and over wastes agent time that could go toward harder problems. This guide walks through activating Zendesk Guide, structuring your Help Center with categories and sections, writing your first articles, and publishing them so customers can find answers on their own.

steps.heading: Activate your Help Center
steps.body: Open the Zendesk products menu and select Guide to turn on your customer-facing self-service portal.

steps.heading: Build your category structure
steps.body: Set up Categories and Sections to organize your knowledge base into logical topics before you start writing content.

steps.heading: Write your first articles
steps.body: Launch the article editor and compose clear titles, bulleted breakdowns, and instructional text for each topic.

steps.heading: Add headings and visuals
steps.body: Break up longer articles with category headings and add screenshots or other visual assets where they help clarify a step.

steps.heading: Set tags and permissions
steps.body: Configure search tags and decide which teams or user segments can view, draft, or publish each article.

steps.heading: Verify and publish
steps.body: Check your brand dashboard to confirm formatting looks correct, then publish the articles so they're live for customers.

faq.question: Do I need a paid Zendesk plan to use Guide?
faq.answer: Guide functionality is tied to your Zendesk subscription plan, so check your account's plan details to confirm what's included.

faq.question: Can I control who sees specific articles?
faq.answer: Yes, Zendesk lets you set visibility permissions so certain articles are restricted to specific agent groups or customer segments.

faq.question: How do I know if my knowledge base articles are helping customers?
faq.answer: Zendesk Guide includes search and article analytics you can review to see which topics customers are searching for and finding useful.

faq.question: Can multiple people write articles at once?
faq.answer: Yes, you can assign draft and publishing permissions to multiple team members so several people can contribute content.
